text.title: Idempotency key contract for write tools
text.summary: How the agent and the tool exchange an idempotency key so retries replay instead of duplicating the side effect.

section.title: The key contract
section.p: An idempotency key is a string (typically a UUID or a deterministic hash of the operation) that the agent generates ONCE per logical write and resends on every retry. The tool stores `key → result` for at least 24h. Second call with same key returns the stored result without running the side effect again.

rule.statement: Every tool whose `execute` mutates external state MUST accept a required `idempotency_key: str` parameter. The tool MUST persist `key → result` for >= 24h and short-circuit on key hit. Reads MUST NOT take the parameter.
rule.rationale: Long agent loops retry on transient tool failures; without a key, the retry cannot be distinguished from a fresh call, and the side effect runs twice. Stripe, AWS Lambda, and Square all ship the same contract for the same reason — at-least-once delivery is the default, exactly-once is a key-store away.

example.note: Replay is cheap, cannot double-refund, and the key is forwarded to Stripe so even the upstream is protected.
If the tool times out after the SMTP server accepted the message, the agent will retry and the user gets two emails.

antipattern.description: Generating the idempotency key INSIDE the tool (`key = uuid4()` at the top of `execute`).
antipattern.reason: The key must be the same across retries. If the tool generates it, every retry gets a fresh key and the dedup never fires.
Bloats the schema, confuses the model, costs tokens — reads are already safe to repeat.

section.reference: Reference Python implementation: Pydantic schema, key-store, replay semantics.
